key_insight: For shallow polynomial neural networks with monomial activations, the loss landscape over an affine target is highly structured: global minima exist only when enough distinct hidden-neuron pivots are active, and for cubic activation the critical points can be completely classified.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a precise rule for when training can succeed or fail by architecture choice, helping diagnose non-attainment, divergence of parameters, and saddle-dominated optimization in small neural models.
method: Analyze the true loss landscape of shallow polynomial neural networks with one-dimensional input/output.
Derive existence and non-existence conditions for global minimizers for arbitrary activation degree d.
Classify all critical points for cubic activation, including minima, saddles, and excluded configurations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical results and complete critical-point classification rather than empirical experiments or benchmark data.
watch_item: The results are specific to shallow one-dimensional polynomial networks with monomial/cubic activations and affine targets, so they may not transfer directly to deeper or higher-dimensional models.
paper_title: Landscape analysis for shallow neural networks: Complete classification of critical points for cubic activation and affine target functions
